paragraphs.en: Readers respond to Melanie Thernstrom’s piece about a concierge women’s-health doctor, Burkhard Bilger’s piece about the lone-star tick, and Adeline Goss’s piece about the potential connections between frontotemporal dementia and criminality.
paragraphs.zh: 读者们对梅拉妮·瑟恩斯特朗（Melanie Thernstrom）关于一位会“提供女性健康会诊”并指导她们优化性高潮的“礼宾式”妇科医生的文章，以及伯克哈德·比格（Burkhard Bilger）关于独星蜱的文章，还有阿德琳·戈斯（Adeline Goss）关于额颞叶痴呆与犯罪可能关联的文章作出回应。

paragraphs.en: It was hard for me to read Melanie Thernstrom’s piece about fabulously wealthy women spending thousands of dollars on a gynecologist who coaches them on optimizing their orgasms, because it arrived the same week that my town’s small community hospital, in southeastern Vermont, announced that it anticipates having to close its birthing center this year (“Billionaires’ Vagina Club,” July 6th & 13th). If that comes to pass, then many women will have to travel out of state, often across long distances, for obstetric care. This week, I also read that the average annual compensation for a C.E.O. of a major national health-insurance company is more than twenty million dollars. I also encountered a poll, no doubt instituted to mark our nation’s two-hundred-and-fiftieth birthday, asking: Are you proud to be an American? I’m thinking about how proud I would be if our country made serious strides to eliminate this level of economic injustice.
paragraphs.zh: 我读梅拉妮·瑟恩斯特朗（Melanie Thernstrom）的那篇文章时很难集中精神。那篇文章讲的是一些极其富有的女性花费数千美元请一位妇产科医生当私人会诊医生，由她指导如何把性高潮“优化到最佳”，而同一周，我所在村镇的一家位于佛蒙特州东南部的小型社区医院宣布，预计今年将不得不关闭其产房（《Billionaires’ Vagina Club》刊于7月6日和13日）。如果此事成真，许多女性就不得不跨州，通常是很长距离外出寻求产科护理。本周我还读到一项消息：一家大型全国性健康保险公司首席执行官的年薪平均超过两千万美元。我还看到一项民意调查，毫无疑问是为了纪念我们国家成立两百五十周年而安排的，问题是：你为自己是美国人而感到骄傲吗？我在想，如果我们的国家能在消除这种程度的经济不公上取得实质进展，我会有多么自豪。
凯伦·泰勒，佛蒙特州 Brattleboro。

paragraphs.en: Burkhard Bilger’s piece about how lone-star ticks are spreading alpha-gal syndrome, a condition that causes meat allergies, is terrifying and informative (“The Great Infestation,” July 6th & 13th). What’s troubling to me, besides the obvious, is the insistence on finding a way to continue to eat animals if your body starts to reject them. “Vegan” may be a curse word to some, but there’s never been a better, and easier, time to be one than now. Brands like Beyond and Impossible make delicious burger patties without meat. Of course, there are critics who say that these products don’t pass their purity tests, but alternative meats have the advantage of not requiring factory farming or breaking you out in a rash. Another plus is that you don’t have to raise cows or pigs only to kill them, just because you prefer the taste of red meat.
paragraphs.zh: 伯克哈德·比格（Burkhard Bilger）关于独星蜱如何传播alpha-gal综合征（该病会引发肉类过敏）的文章，既令人恐惧又信息量十足（《The Great Infestation》，7月6日和13日）。除了显而易见的问题之外，我更担心的是，人们还在坚持要找到一种方式，使得即使身体开始排斥动物，也能继续吃动物。对一些人来说，“Vegan”可能是个脏词，但如今成为“素食者”前所未有、也更容易。像Beyond和Impossible这样的品牌能做出不含肉类的美味汉堡饼。自然，也有人批评这些产品不过关，无法通过他们心中的“纯净”标准，但替代肉至少有一个优势：它不需要工厂化养殖，也不会让你长满荨麻疹。另一个好处是，你不必为了爱吃红肉而先养牛羊猪再杀掉它们。
罗伯特·霍华德（Robert Howard），肯塔基州麦迪逊维尔。

paragraphs.en: Bilger’s article struck a chord with me. Fifty years ago, when I was thirty, I passed out with a swollen face and hives a few hours after eating prime rib at a celebratory dinner. My family doctor, in Tennessee, thought I had an allergic reaction, but he couldn’t tell what had triggered it, and implored me to see a specialist. All the allergy tests doctors later performed came back negative. It was only after I started eliminating foods that I realized my reactions were caused by red meat.
paragraphs.zh: 比尔格（Bilger）的文章让我很有共鸣。五十年前我三十岁时，庆祝晚宴上吃了块上好的牛排，几个小时后我就脸肿、起了荨麻疹晕倒。我的家庭医生在田纳西州时认为是过敏反应，但他不知道是什么引起的，并劝我去看专科医生。后来所有医生做的过敏测试都显示阴性。直到我开始逐一排除食物之后，才意识到自己的反应是由红肉引起的。

paragraphs.en: Many years later, I was diagnosed with alpha-gal syndrome. My journey with this condition has entailed visiting the emergency room three times, always carrying an EpiPen, travelling with a card that describes my allergy in several languages, and constantly alerting restaurant chefs and staff that I can have no food that might contain meat by-products or be contaminated by them. Last year, after a lovely dinner in San Francisco that I thought was safe, I was in an emergency room explaining alpha-gal to doctors who had never even heard of it.
paragraphs.zh: 许多年后，我被诊断出α-半乳糖苷血清反应（alpha-gal综合征）。我与这种病的相处包括去过三次急诊室，随时携带肾上腺素自动注射器（EpiPen），随身带着一张用多种语言写的过敏说明卡，并且始终提醒餐厅厨师和服务员，我不能吃任何可能含有肉类副产品或可能被其污染的食物。去年，在旧金山我本以为很安全的一次愉快晚餐后，我仍被送进了急诊室，向从未听说过alpha-gal的医生解释这种过敏。

paragraphs.en: I am waiting for the day when there might be a preventive remedy. In the meantime, I appreciate the exposure The New Yorker has given to this unusual allergy, which is prevalent in the South and now moving across the country.
paragraphs.zh: 我在等待某一天能出现预防性疗法。与此同时，我很感激《纽约客》对这种不寻常过敏的关注。这种过敏在美国南方很常见，而且正在向全国扩散。
莎拉·亨特，美国田纳西州纳什维尔

paragraphs.en: I was troubled by Adeline Goss’s recent article about Ray Howell, a doctor whose criminal actions may have been caused by frontotemporal dementia (Annals of Science, newyorker.com/goss-on-ray-howell, June 11th). Howell’s F.T.D. was linked to a genetic mutation known as a C9orf72 (C9) hexanucleotide repeat expansion. I write from a place of concern for the well-being of those living with this mutation, as I fear that many readers will be left with the impression that there is strong evidence that having it is definitively and consistently associated with profoundly negative characteristics, including criminality. As a statistician who has partnered with A.L.S./F.T.D. advocacy groups and researchers to interrogate the published literature on the genetically at-risk community, I believe that the evidence does not support this impression.
paragraphs.zh: 我对阿德琳·戈斯最近发表的关于雷伊·豪威尔（Ray Howell）的文章感到困扰，该文章讨论了这位医生的犯罪行为可能由额叶颞叶痴呆（FTD）引起（《Annals of Science》，newyorker.com/goss-on-ray-howell，6 月 11 日）。豪威尔的额颞叶痴呆与一种被称为 C9orf72（C9）六核苷酸重复扩增的遗传突变有关。写下这封信，是出于我对携带该突变者福祉的关注，因为我担心许多读者会因此留下这样的印象，即有强有力的证据表明，携带该突变与一系列极其负面的特征（包括犯罪）有明确且一致的关联。作为一名统计学家，我曾与 ALS/FTD 维权组织和研究者合作，系统审视过该高风险基因群体相关的公开文献，并认为现有证据并不支持这种印象。

paragraphs.en: Goss cites a study that found half of F.T.D. patients who were C9-mutation carriers had fixed behavioral patterns, like rigid meal schedules and excessive exercise routines. Research indicates that the majority of mutation carriers will never develop F.T.D., however, so it is unclear how these findings would apply to the broader C9-carrier population. Furthermore, this study should not be used to draw conclusions because its sample size for carriers was very small: only twenty. The authors also describe exercising more than four times a week as excessive. Surely most people would agree that four times a week is not necessarily excessive. What’s more, the authors do not present any formal analysis for the observation, which is based on relatives’ accounts, that the study’s subjects became (as Goss quotes) “bad tempered” if they couldn’t exercise. Finally, the paper acknowledges that the study’s interviewer was not blind to the C9 status of its participants and went in with a specific hypothesis—circumstances that risk bias.
paragraphs.zh: 戈斯引用了一项研究：其中一半的 C9 突变携带者且患有 FTD 的患者表现出固定行为模式，例如进食时间过于僵化和过度的运动计划。然而，研究也指出，大多数突变携带者永远不会发展为 FTD，因此这些发现是否能够适用于更广泛的 C9 携带者人群并不清楚。此外，这项研究的携带者样本量非常小：只有二十人，因此不应据此得出结论。作者还把每周锻炼超过四次定义为“过度”。毫无疑问，大多数人会同意，每周四次并不一定算过度。更进一步，作者没有对该观察做任何正式统计分析，而这一观察基于亲属叙述：研究对象如果不能运动就会“脾气暴躁”（正如戈斯所引述）。最后，论文也承认，研究访谈者并不知道受访者的 C9 状态，也未对其保持盲法，且带着特定假设去访谈——这些情形都可能产生偏差。

paragraphs.en: More broadly, it’s also worth remembering how far Dr. Howell’s actions are from the norm: even among C9 carriers who do develop F.T.D., serious crimes of this kind are rare. We should be careful how we communicate the implications of C9 mutations so as not to stigmatize carriers, or discourage them from participating in research or getting clinical care. C9 carriers deserve to be treated with respect—and to know that a sequence in their DNA does not define who they are.
paragraphs.zh: 更广泛地说，也应当记住豪威尔医生的行为与常态相去甚远：即使是在确实发展为 FTD 的 C9 携带者中，这类严重犯罪也很少见。我们在传播 C9 突变含义时应当谨慎，以免对携带者形成污名化，或让他们不愿参与研究、拒绝接受临床照护。C9 携带者应当得到尊重，也应该知道：他们体内的 DNA 序列并不定义他们是谁。
丹尼尔·布里克曼，加州旧金山。

paragraphs.en: Letters should be sent with the writer’s name, address, and daytime phone number via e-mail to themail@newyorker.com. Letters may be edited for length and clarity, and may be published in any medium. We regret that owing to the volume of correspondence we cannot reply to every letter.
paragraphs.zh: 来信请附上作者姓名、住址和白天联系电话，通过电子邮件发送至 themail@newyorker.com。来信可能会进行长度和表达上的修改，并可在任何媒介上发表。我们很遗憾，由于来信量巨大，无法回复每一封信件。
